seoTitle: Frankfield vs Fraser Island — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Frankfield and Fraser Island. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Frankfield vs Fraser Island: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Frankfield and Fraser Island.
heading: Frankfield vs Fraser Island — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Frankfield, Queensland and Fraser Island, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Frankfield has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Fraser Island (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Frankfield or Fraser Island safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Frankfield has a flood risk score of 32/100, while Fraser Island scores 55/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Frankfield scores 30/100 for bushfire risk. Fraser Island scores 55/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Frankfield or Fraser Island?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Frankfield has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
